qa_pairs.question: How do you assign a public IP address to a virtual machine (VM) in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: To assign a public IP address (also known as a Floating IP) to your VM in OpenStack, first ensure you are viewing the instances list. Click the drop-down arrow menu on the far right of the row for your VM and select "Associate Floating IP." In the "Allocate Floating IP" window, click the "Allocate IP" button. If this is your first time associating a floating IP, you'll need to click the "+" sign in the "Manage Floating IP Associations" dialog box first. After allocating, click the "Associate" button. Your VM will then have two IP addresses: a private one (e.g., 192.168.X.Y) and the new public IP, which is essential for connecting to your VM. You can also view a list of public IP addresses and their associated projects by going to "Network->Floating IPs" in the OpenStack menu.

qa_pairs.question: What is the initial step to configure the firewall settings for a virtual machine in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: To configure the firewall settings for your virtual machine in OpenStack, the initial step is to select "Network->Security Groups" from the OpenStack left menu. Then, on the row labelled "default" for the security group, click on the "Manage Rules" button located on the far right.
